title: Rich preview fails with CloudFront error for shared occurrence links
description: Sharing https://salishsea.io/?o=inaturalist:377539157 to Facebook renders a broken preview: 'ERROR: The request could not be satisfied' (the standard CloudFront error page). This is the Lambda@Edge OG-meta rich-preview path (docs/decisions/002), which is specified to FAIL OPEN (pass through on any error) — so either that regressed or the failure is a Lambda execution error/timeout CloudFront surfaces as 502/504. Hypothesis to check first: breakage is specific to iNaturalist-prefixed occurrence IDs (inaturalist:NNN) — iNat is modeled-but-export-excluded per SRC-01 (decision 005), so the edge lookup may hit a code path that throws instead of failing open. Repro: crawler user-agent (facebookexternalhit) against the ?o= URL.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (diagnosed, not iNat-specific): the Lambda@Edge handler (infra/lib/edge-handler/index.ts) intercepts crawler requests on ALL paths except its bypass list (/dwca/, /sitemap.xml, /robots.txt — lines 119-125). og:image is https://salishsea.io/preview.jpg (FALLBACK_IMAGE, line 106). When Facebook's crawler fetches that image URL, the handler has no ?o=, so it returns generic OG *HTML* (lines 137-143) instead of the JPEG. Confirmed: /preview.jpg returns image/jpeg 168717B to a browser UA but text/html 896B to facebookexternalhit. Blast radius: every preview whose og:image falls back to preview.jpg — the generic site card AND any occurrence with no open-licensed (cc0/cc-by) photo, which includes this iNat river-otter. Occurrences with an external open photo URL have an off-host og:image and are unaffected, which is why it wasn't universally caught. FIX: add static image assets to the pre-bot bypass so crawlers always get raw asset bytes (e.g. bypass when request.uri matches /\.(jpe?g|png|gif|svg|webp|ico|avif)$/i). Note: index.test.ts lines 94-162 assert og:image==preview.jpg but never assert preview.jpg itself serves an image — add a regression test that a crawler request to /preview.jpg passes through (returns the request, not synthesized HTML).
Fixed via PR #299 (merged to main, deployed by GH Actions run 28626380144, both Build+Deploy green). Verified live 2026-07-02: /preview.jpg returns image/jpeg 168717B to facebookexternalhit (was text/html 896B); occurrence OG HTML still generated correctly; home page still intercepted. Remaining human step: re-scrape shared links in FB Sharing Debugger to bust cache.

title: Expand scope from cetaceans to all Salish Sea marine mammals (PSEMP)
description:
PRODUCT.md and docs/decisions/001 currently scope the platform to cetaceans only; actual intent is all Salish Sea marine mammals per the PSEMP Marine Mammals Workgroup hub (https://psemp-marinemammalsworkgroup-wa-psp.hub.arcgis.com/): cetaceans + pinnipeds (harbor seal, CA/Steller sea lion, N elephant seal) + otters (sea + marine-foraging river otter). The Lutrinae taxon already hand-added to the iNaturalist query is an early instance.

Open decisions (for a grill-with-docs session, not this migration PR):
- Does 'include everything from PSEMP' mean widen species scope only, or also ingest PSEMP's ArcGIS datasets as a new provider/collection?
- Product identity: reframe as marine-mammal platform, or whale-centric with mammals included?
- Rights: PSEMP data is CC-BY-NC-SA; ShareAlike may be incompatible with the CC-BY-NC export contract (docs/rights-policy.md) if PSEMP records are ingested + redistributed. Resolve at the policy gate before any SQL.

Doc updates gated on the above: PRODUCT.md (What This Is, core value, Out of Scope), supersede docs/decisions/001, CONTEXT.md (model pinnipeds/otters; 'occurrence' currently cetacean-defined; segment travel-speed heuristics don't generalize to hauled-out seals). Implementation gaps: taxon filters across all four ingest sources; species/UI; export attribution + SRC-01-style logic for any PSEMP provider.

title: Discovery conversations with Orca Network (Garrett & Berta)
description: The community-uptake direction rests on a partner we don't yet understand — next move is discovery, not engineering. Interview questions: docs/strategy/orca-network-discovery-questions.md (curation workflow & pain; would they value/advocate a map-first tool; Acartia data-flow granularity; does the research-impact story land; what would make them say no).

title: Community uptake via Orca Network partnership
description: Path to first-party submissions: partnership with Orca Network, targeting the shore-regular persona. The wedge is doing their FB sighting-coordination function properly (map-first, structured, trajectory-aware). Lead with the partnership, not scraping. See docs/decisions/007-community-uptake-strategy.md and docs/strategy/community-uptake.md.

title: Emit edge-handler build output outside the source tree (stop shipping tests to Lambda@Edge)
description:
infra/lib/infra-stack.ts:33 deploys the edge handler via lambda.Code.fromAsset('edge-handler'), which bundles the WHOLE directory to Lambda@Edge — including index.test.ts/.js and .d.ts. Two problems: (1) test files ship to a Lambda@Edge viewer trigger, which has a hard 1MB code limit; (2) tsc emits compiled JS next to sources, which shadowed the source under jest until moduleFileExtensions was fixed (see PR #299 / gh-299).

Fix options: emit to a dist/ outDir and point Code.fromAsset at dist/edge-handler, OR bundle the handler with esbuild at synth time so the source stays .ts-only and no committed/emitted JS is needed. Either way the deployed asset should contain only the handler runtime, not tests or sources.

Acceptance: cdk synth succeeds; the synthesized Lambda asset excludes test files and .ts sources; edge-handler jest suite still green. Add a check (test or synth assertion) that the asset contains index.js and not *.test.*.

title: Human review of rights-policy advisories
description: Three advisories from the v1.2 policy gate verification remain human_needed (Sections 1-2 completeness, Section 4 conferral framing, Section 3 gap coverage). All auto checks green. Policy now lives at docs/rights-policy.md.

title: Emit coordinateUncertaintyInMeters in DwC-A export
description: GBIF validator flagged absence (2026-06-19). Needs a per-channel source-of-uncertainty decision; touches scripts/dwca/fields.ts ordering, which ripples to meta.xml and GeoParquet. Policy: emit when known, omit when NULL, never 0 (docs/rights-policy.md).

title: Enrich eml.xml resource contacts and re-check GBIF validator
description: GBIF RESOURCE_CONTACTS_MISSING_OR_INCOMPLETE (2026-06-19). v1.3 Phase 13 added a resource contact — re-validate to confirm the warning cleared.

title: Ingest in-region Orca sightings from GBIF
description: Pull Salish Sea cetacean records already in GBIF from datasets we don't otherwise ingest; surface in-region ones. Mirror-image of SRC-01 (docs/decisions/005): must dedupe against provider + source_url to avoid re-importing our own contributed records. Was GSD backlog phase 999.2.

title: Surface sightings from Facebook community groups
description: Cold-start / lock-in mitigation. Partnership-first per decision 007 — scraping is the fallback, not the lead. Scope depends on discovery outcome.

title: Make the sighter's contribution visible
description: Thin UI over the existing v1.2/v1.3 pipeline giving a sighter immediate proof their observation counts (lands in the record, feeds Acartia→WRAS and nightly DwC-A→GBIF). Open: unit of reward (per-sighting confirmation / running count / personal map layer), tie to auth identity, how loud. Partially seeded by sidebar pitch (commit f014316). Sequence after map is populated and Orca Network direction confirmed.

title: Confirm Facebook preview renders after cache expiry (og:image cache-bust ready if not)
description:
The /preview.jpg content-type bug is fixed and deployed (see closed salishsea-io-i5u / PR #299): the edge function (viewer-request, v9) now passes image assets through to S3, verified serving image/jpeg from all UAs at SEA POP; CloudFront invalidation I9G6C691FCAJHTK06IBVXUVTKF completed. Remaining symptom is Facebook's OWN sticky image cache still holding the old 'invalid content type' verdict for https://salishsea.io/preview.jpg — not our infra (viewer-request responses are never CF-cached, so no stale POP copy existed).

NEXT: re-run FB Sharing Debugger 'Scrape Again' after FB's image cache expires (hours–~1 day). If it renders, close this.

IF STILL BROKEN: cache-bust og:image so FB treats it as a new resource — change FALLBACK_IMAGE in infra/lib/edge-handler/index.ts from 'https://salishsea.io/preview.jpg' to '...preview.jpg?v=2' (passes through fine: carve-out matches on path, S3 ignores the query string) and update the two og:image assertions in index.test.ts. One-line follow-up PR.

title: Contextual data enriching a sighting
description: Sighter sees nearby historical sightings, salmon run data, tides, individual whale biographical info alongside their sighting.

title: Link to external cetacean resources
description: Platform links out to existing external cetacean resources and databases.

title: Emit organismID and register archive with GBIF/OBIS
description: Occurrence records carry individual-animal identity (organismID); register the DwC-A with GBIF/OBIS. Note: unvalidated whale IDs (e.g. T065S) are never emitted as organismID per docs/rights-policy.md — only validated identities qualify.

title: Catalog of individual Salish Sea cetaceans
description: Platform hosts a comprehensive catalog of individual cetaceans (all species). HappyWhale tracks 693 individuals — organismID territory, distinct from contributors (INDIV-01).

title: App UI: organization/collection browse pages
description: Frontend surfaces for the v1.3 provenance graph — deferred from v1.3 by design (backend-only milestone).

title: Direct partner ingest: OrcaSound / HappyWhale
description: Slot new partners into the collections model (org row + collection row + ingest writer setting collection_id); no schema change per partner. Fresh question per partner: do they forward observer names / stable IDs for recordedByID? Trigger: partner confirms API access, or product wants to retire Maplify.

title: Cross-provider contributor unification (contributor_links)
description: jmaughn (iNat) ≈ James Maughn (native) is probable, not confirmed. Extension point is a contributor_links table — never a shared FK on name similarity (docs/decisions/006).

title: URL → whole-occurrence importer (source_url Layer 2)
description: Given a source_url, fetch and map a whole occurrence (species/date/location/photos/observer), not just resolve its channel. One fetcher per provider domain; reuse the URL registry; dedupe on source_url; respect SRC-01. Trigger: appetite for a new inbound ingest path.

title: Populate contributor ORCIDs for 28 native contributors
description: contributors.orcid column and recordedByID emit shipped in v1.3; manual curation of the 28 native contributors remains.
